scope: Full capture-page state audit and minimal parameter state-priority closure

conflicting_state: runtime AUTO preset callbacks cleared pending manual values while manual device writes were still in flight
WB was most exposed because white-balance gains are confirmed asynchronously after device configuration
temporary_patches_reviewed: R62/R63 manual takeover target logic retained
R63 DEBUG simulator fallback retained and confirmed DEBUG/simulator-only

root_cause.wb_first_drag: A manual WB drag established pendingWhiteBalanceWheelValue, but selectedWhiteBalancePreset could still emit or remain .auto before the async device write confirmed .custom. The previous onChange handler cleared pending immediately on .auto, allowing UI to fall back to AUTO and making the first drag appear swallowed.
root_cause.category: state-priority conflict between pending manual UI state and runtime AUTO echo

capture_screen: Do not clear WB pending manual state when runtime AUTO is observed while pendingWhiteBalanceWheelValue exists.
Do not clear ISO pending manual state when runtime AUTO is observed while pendingISOWheelValue exists.
Do not clear Shutter pending manual state when runtime AUTO is observed while pendingShutterWheelDurationSeconds exists.
Explicit AUTO button paths still clear pending before calling runtime AUTO.

contracts.wb: runtime contract unchanged; pending manual now outranks runtime AUTO echo
contracts.tint: unchanged; RESET remains RESET and explicit WB AUTO still clears Tint
contracts.iso: runtime contract unchanged; pending manual now outranks runtime AUTO echo
contracts.shutter: runtime contract unchanged; pending manual now outranks runtime AUTO echo

validation.xcodebuild_note: Xcode emitted an existing IDEFileReferenceDebug warning but ended with BUILD SUCCEEDED.

risks: WB first right-drag still requires real-device validation.
If the issue persists, inspect DEBUG WB logs for timeout or another state callback clearing pending.
manualTakeoverTargetIndex remains somewhat complex but was not rewritten to avoid broad behavior changes.
